back_translation: When the disturbance had calmed down, Paul sent for the disciples. After encouraging them, he said farewell and departed for the province of Macedonia.
After traveling through those regions and encouraging them with many words, he arrived at the province of Greece,
He stayed there for three months. As he was about to sail to the province of Syria, some Jews plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of the province of Macedonia.

Those who traveled with him were Sopater son of Pyrrhus, a man of the city of Berea; Aristarchus and Secundus, men of the city of Thessalonica; Gaius, a man of the city of Derbe; Timothy; and Tychicus and Trophimus, men of the province of Asia.
These men went on ahead and waited for us at the city of Troas.
As for us, after the Festival of Unleavened Bread, we sailed from the city of Philippi. Five days later we met them at the city of Troas, and we stayed there seven days.
On the first day of the week, we gathered together to break bread. Paul conversed with them, and because he intended to depart the next day, he prolonged his speech until midnight.
In the upper room where we had gathered, there were many oil lamps.

A young man named Eutychus was sitting at the window opening. As Paul kept on speaking, he began to grow drowsy until he fell soundly asleep, and then fell down from the third story. When they lifted him up, they found that he had died.
Paul went down, threw himself upon him and embraced him, and said, 'Do not be alarmed, for his life is still in him.'
Then Paul went back upstairs, broke bread and ate. After conversing on until daybreak, he departed.
They brought the young man home alive, and were greatly comforted.
As for us, we went on ahead to the ship and sailed to the city of Assos, intending to take Paul on board there, because he had arranged it that way, since he intended to walk on foot himself.
When he met us at the city of Assos, we took him on board and sailed on to the city of Mitylene.
The next day we sailed from there and arrived opposite the island of Chios. The day after that we put in at the island of Samos, and on the following day we came to the city of Miletus.

Paul had decided to sail past the city of Ephesus so he would not have to spend time in the province of Asia, because he was hurrying to reach Jerusalem in time for the Festival of Pentecost, if possible.
Paul sent from the city of Miletus to the city of Ephesus to invite the elders of the church to come and meet him.
When they arrived to him, he said to them, 'You yourselves know well, from the first day I set foot in the province of Asia, how I lived among you the whole time I was there,
serving the Lord with all humility, with tears, and amid the trials that came upon me through the schemes of certain Jews.
I did not hold back anything that was profitable to you, but proclaimed and taught you both publicly and from house to house,
testifying to both Jews and Greeks of repentance toward God and faith in our Lord Jesus.
Now, behold, I am bound by the Spirit and am going to Jerusalem, not knowing what will happen to me there.
I only know that the Holy Spirit testifies to me in every city, saying that chains and afflictions await me.

But I do not consider my life to be of any value to myself; only that I may run to the end of my course and complete the ministry I received from the Lord Jesus, namely to bear witness to the gospel of the grace of God.
Now, behold, I know that all of you among whom I have gone proclaiming the kingdom of God will not see my face again.
Therefore I declare to you this day that I am free from guilt of the blood of all.
For I did not hold back from declaring to you the entire counsel of God.
Take heed to yourselves and to the whole flock, in which the Holy Spirit has appointed you as overseers, to shepherd the church of God, which he obtained as his own with his own blood.
I know that after my departure, fierce wolves will come in among you and will not spare the flock,
and from among yourselves, men will arise speaking distorted things to draw the disciples away after them.
Therefore be on watch, and remember that for three years, both day and night, I never stopped warning each one with tears.
Now I commit you to God and to the word of his grace, which is able to build you up and to give an inheritance among all those whom he has sanctified.
I have not coveted anyone's silver, gold, or clothing.
You yourselves know well that these two hands worked to provide for the needs of myself and of those who were with me.
In all things, I showed you that working hard like this we must help the weak, and remember the words of the Lord Jesus himself who said, 'It is more blessed to give than to receive.'
When he had said these things, Paul knelt down and prayed together with all of them.
All of them wept much. They embraced Paul and kissed him farewell,
grieving most of all over the words he had spoken, that they would no longer see his face. Then they sent him on his way to the ship.
